summary: Gruber notes that Apple's RAM pricing for the new MacBooks and MacBook Pros is, surprisingly, competitive with third-party dealers like Crucial, OWC, and Newegg. Historically, Apple has charged two to three times market price for memory upgrades, making third-party purchasing the standard advice. The 2 GB to 4 GB upgrade costs $150 from Apple versus $135-$160 from competitors, making it essentially a wash after shipping. However, Gruber warns this may not last as commodity prices fluctuate. He contrasts this with Apple's still-egregious pricing on other machines, particularly the Mac Pro where Apple charges $9,100 for a memory upgrade that costs $1,680 from OWC.
key_points: Apple's RAM upgrade pricing for the new MacBooks is nearly identical to third-party dealers, a historic first
The standard advice to buy RAM separately and install it yourself may not apply to the new MacBook line
Apple's competitive pricing likely won't last as commodity RAM prices fluctuate while Apple's stay fixed
Apple's RAM pricing on other Macs remains exorbitant — the Mac Pro markup is so extreme you could buy a second maxed-out Mac Pro with the savings
money_quotes: One of the longest-standing truisms in buying a Mac is that you don't get competitive prices when buying memory upgrades direct from Apple.
Historically, Apple has charged exorbitant prices for RAM upgrades in build-your-own configurations — upwards of two or three times the market price, sometimes even higher.
My assumption has always been that Apple could get away with such pricing because many Mac users just aren't comfortable installing RAM chips by hand.
The most egregious example is the Mac Pro. Upgrading to the maximum memory configuration of 32 GB from Apple will cost you $9,100; the same upgrade from OWC costs just $1,680.
A difference so large that you could buy a second Mac Pro also maxed out to 32 GB with the savings.
key_insight: Apple's historically predatory RAM pricing has become surprisingly competitive on the new MacBooks, though the rest of the Mac lineup remains as overpriced as ever.
